content:
Grateful to share that I secured the Top Position at the 2nd International E-Conference on World Constitutional Law, Governance & Justice 2026, organized by the Institute of Excellence in Research and Law (I.E.R.L.).
My research paper, "Sanskritization and Legal Affirmative Action under Articles 15 and 16," was recognized with a Certificate of Excellence. 

This conference involved a paper presentation as well. The presentation was quite interactive and gave me exposure to a spectrum of opinions and emerging research areas. This experience reinforced my interest in constitutional law and the role of legal research in addressing questions of equality and social justice. I am looking forward to learning, researching, and contributing more.
comment: An important dimension is how constitutional equality debates balance formal legal principles with lived social realities. The real challenge lies in translating research into fair, workable policy outcomes.
